title: High Protein Chicken Taco Soup (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: High Protein Chicken Taco Soup is a comforting bowl built around lean chicken, beans, and warm taco spices. It is easy to cook in batches and simple to log in Eati for accurate calories and protein.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken (breast or chopped)
- Onion and garlic
- Taco seasoning
- Chicken broth
- Diced tomatoes
- Black beans or beans of choice
- Corn
- Lime
- Optional: chopped cilantro, jalapeno, and a light topping

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Cook onion and garlic in a pot until fragrant.
2. Add chicken and taco seasoning, then stir until coated.
3. Pour in broth and tomatoes; simmer until the chicken is cooked through.
4. Stir in beans and corn and simmer a few more minutes.
5. Finish with lime juice and serve. Log your portion in Eati to dial in your macros.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 463 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 9.8g
Protein: 47g

Tip: choose a lighter topping to keep fat lower while preserving flavor.

sections.heading: Why Chicken Taco Soup Is a Weight-Loss Powerhouse
sections.content:
At 463 calories with 47g of protein and under 10g of fat, this taco soup is a nutritional masterpiece. Few meals achieve this protein-to-calorie efficiency.

Why soups work so well for fat loss:
• High water content = high fullness — research shows soup consumption before meals reduces total calorie intake by 20%.
• Slow eating — liquid requires spooning and time, preventing overeating.
• Volume amplification — a big bowl feels satisfying for relatively few calories.
• Long staying power — protein + fiber + volume keeps you full 5–6 hours.

Macro breakdown per serving:
• Chicken breast (5 oz shredded): 230 cal, 45g protein.
• Black beans (1/2 cup): 110 cal, 7g protein, 7g fiber.
• Corn (1/4 cup): 35 cal.
• Diced tomatoes + broth: 50 cal.
• Onion, garlic, spices: 40 cal.

Compare to a single chicken taco at Chipotle (~450 cal, 20g protein) or a bowl of chili at Wendy's (~240 cal, 20g protein). This homemade soup wins on both calories and protein.

Use a protein calculator to see how this hits your daily target.

sections.heading: Taco Seasoning: Homemade vs Store-Bought
sections.content:
Commercial taco seasoning packets are convenient but often packed with sodium (600mg+ per teaspoon) and cornstarch fillers. Making your own takes 90 seconds and tastes better.

Homemade taco seasoning (2 tbsp, enough for 1 batch):
• 2 tsp chili powder
• 1.5 tsp cumin
• 1 tsp paprika (or smoked paprika)
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1/2 tsp oregano
• 1/2 tsp black pepper
• 1/2 tsp salt (adjust to taste)
• Optional: 1/4 tsp cayenne for heat

Bonus: This same blend works for burrito bowls, fajitas, chili, and ground beef.

Making it bulk:
Multiply quantities by 6 and store in an airtight jar. Makes about 1 cup of seasoning — enough for 6+ recipes. Costs under $2 to make vs $6+ for 6 store packets.

Sodium control: Use 1 tsp salt instead of the 2 tsp equivalent in commercial packets. Saves 500mg sodium per batch — meaningful for blood pressure management.

sections.heading: Smart Toppings: Adding Flavor Without Calorie Bombs
sections.content:
Toppings make or break this soup. The wrong ones can double the calories; the right ones amplify flavor for almost free.

Near-free toppings (under 15 cal):
• Fresh cilantro (0 cal)
• Lime wedges (0 cal)
• Diced red onion (5 cal per tbsp)
• Pickled jalapeños (5 cal)
• Hot sauce (0 cal)
• Fresh diced tomatoes (5 cal per tbsp)

Modest additions (20–50 cal):
• 2 tbsp Greek yogurt instead of sour cream (20 cal, 3g protein)
• 1 tbsp shredded cheddar (30 cal)
• 1/4 oz broken tortilla chips (30 cal) — crushed, adds crunch
• 1 tbsp pico de gallo (10 cal)

Higher-calorie but worth it (50–100 cal):
• 1/4 sliced avocado (80 cal, adds healthy fats and creaminess)
• 2 tbsp guacamole (50 cal)
• 1/2 oz baked tortilla strips (50 cal)

Avoid or minimize (100+ cal):
• Full-fat sour cream (60 cal per 2 tbsp)
• Lots of shredded cheese (115 cal per 1/4 cup)
• Handfuls of tortilla chips (150+ cal per oz)
• Fried taco shells (150 cal each)

Best combo for fat loss: Greek yogurt + cilantro + lime + hot sauce + 1/4 avocado = 100 calories of pure flavor. Keeps soup under 565 cal total.

sections.heading: Lightening and Boosting: Adjusting Macros
sections.content:
To lower calories (–80 per serving):
• Use canned chicken (rinsed, lean) instead of fresh for speed and lower fat.
• Reduce beans to 1/3 cup per serving.
• Skip corn or use frozen sparingly.
• New total: ~380 cal per serving with 47g protein.

To add more protein (+10g per serving):
• Add 1/4 cup cottage cheese blended into the base.
• Stir in 1 scoop unflavored whey protein (if blended smooth it disappears).
• Add extra chicken breast per batch.

To add volume/fiber without calories:
• Add 2 cups chopped kale or spinach in the last 5 minutes.
• Bulk with diced zucchini or yellow squash.
• Add canned no-salt-added diced tomatoes for extra volume.

Low-sodium version:
• Use low-sodium chicken broth.
• Rinse canned beans thoroughly (cuts sodium by 40%).
• Homemade taco seasoning with reduced salt.
• Saves 500+mg sodium per serving.

All variations track easily in Eati — just describe the version you made.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Freezing, and Serving Ideas
sections.content:
Taco soup is arguably the ultimate meal prep food — it reheats perfectly, freezes beautifully, and flavor peaks on day 2.

Batch cooking:
• Double or triple the recipe in a large pot or slow cooker.
• Makes 8–12 servings that last a week plus freezer backup.
• Total active time for a double batch: ~15 minutes.

Storage:
• Fridge: 5 days in airtight containers (flavor peaks day 2–3).
• Freezer: 3 months. Freeze in 2-cup individual portions for quick single-serving thaws.

Reheating:
• Stovetop (best): Medium-low, 6–8 minutes, stir occasionally.
• Microwave: 2 minutes covered, stir, 60 more seconds.
• Slow cooker (for batch warming): Low for 90 minutes.

Creative serving ideas:
• Traditional bowl: With Greek yogurt, cilantro, avocado, lime.
• Taco bowl-style: Over 1/2 cup cauliflower rice.
• As a dip/filling: Reduce slightly and stuff into bell peppers or baked sweet potatoes.
• Chicken tortilla soup variant: Add baked tortilla strips on top.
• With jasmine rice: 1/4 cup cooked (~50 cal) for a more filling version.

Track portions carefully: The variability in bean and corn servings can swing calories by 50+ per bowl. Weighing or measuring once helps lock in accurate macros. See [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly) for more.

conclusion: This taco soup is an effortless way to get a high-protein dinner that still feels comforting. Use Eati to track servings and keep your calorie deficit working.
metaTitle: High Protein Chicken Taco Soup: 47g Protein in 463 Calories (Easy Batch Recipe)
metaDescription: High-protein chicken taco soup delivers 47g of protein in 463 calories per serving. Includes homemade taco seasoning, smart toppings, lightening tips, and meal prep strategies.

faqs.question: How many calories in chicken taco soup?
faqs.answer: This version: approximately 463 calories per serving with 47g of protein and 10g of fat. Restaurant versions can run 600–900 calories with lower protein. Using light toppings (Greek yogurt instead of sour cream, no cheese) keeps this recipe well under 500 calories.

faqs.question: Is chicken taco soup healthy for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's ideal for weight loss. High protein (47g), high fiber (from beans), low fat (10g), and high water content for satiety. Soups consistently outperform equivalent-calorie solid meals for fullness and reduced total calorie intake.

faqs.question: Can I make chicken taco soup in an Instant Pot?
faqs.answer: Yes. Add all ingredients except corn; pressure cook on high for 12 minutes with natural release. Shred chicken, add corn, and sauté for 3 minutes to heat through. Total time: about 35 minutes vs 45+ for stovetop.

faqs.question: How do I thicken chicken taco soup?
faqs.answer: Options: (1) Remove 1 cup of beans and broth, blend smooth, stir back in. (2) Add 1 tbsp masa harina (traditional) — only 30 calories and gives authentic texture. (3) Reduce by simmering uncovered for 10 extra minutes. (4) Add 2 tbsp mashed beans mid-cook.

faqs.question: How long does chicken taco soup last?
faqs.answer: 5 days in the fridge in airtight containers, or 3 months in the freezer. Flavor peaks on days 2–3 as the spices continue to develop. Freeze in individual portions for quick single-serving meals.
